title: Renewable vs. Nonrenewable Energy Flow
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive network diagram comparing renewable and nonrenewable energy pathways from original source to end use

learningObjectives: Compare renewable and nonrenewable energy pathways from source to end use
Explain that renewable vs. nonrenewable describes replenishment rate, not the fundamental source
Trace energy transformations from original source through to human use
prerequisites: Basic understanding of energy forms (kinetic, thermal, chemical, radiant)
Awareness of common energy sources used by humans

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Visual network tracing and pathway comparison
keyQuestions: Where does the energy in coal originally come from?
What makes an energy source renewable versus nonrenewable?
How many transformation steps does solar energy undergo before reaching your home?
Why is biomass renewable but fossil fuels are not, if both come from the Sun?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think renewable means 'infinite' rather than 'replenished on human timescales'
Students may not realize that fossil fuels are stored ancient solar energy
Students may believe geothermal energy comes from the Sun
assessmentOpportunities: Students complete a pathway-tracing table for all energy sources
Students draw their own energy pathway diagram for their home
Class discussion on the definition and nuance of 'renewable'
